3.name: Area Protocol
3.html:
Your Suppression Fire technique is enhanced: - Its area of effect extends to 30-foot-wide. - The resistance check DC is now your Weave-equivalent DC (8 + proficiency + Wisdom modifier). - Creatures who fail the check also have -3 to Attack cards on their first attack (not just movement reduction).

Additionally, you can maintain Suppression Fire as a concentration effect (up to 1 minute), re-establishing the fire area at the start of each of your turns without using your action.

6.name: Zone Control
6.html: You can designate a Kill Zone — a 30-foot radius area within your weapon's normal range. As a bonus action, you establish the Kill Zone. While active (up to 1 minute, concentration): - Any creature that moves more than 15 feet in a single turn within the Kill Zone takes 2d8 damage. - Creatures that end their turn in the Kill Zone must make an EDGE resistance check (DC = 8 + proficiency + Wisdom modifier) or be unable to take the Dash action on their next turn.

10.name: Full Suppression
10.html:
Once per short rest, as an action, you can execute a Full Suppression — a comprehensive discharge that locks down a 60-foot radius area for 1 round. All creatures in the area (friend and foe) make EDGE resistance checks (DC = 8 + proficiency + Wisdom modifier). On a failure: - They take 4d8 resonant damage. - They cannot take reactions until the start of their next turn. - They cannot move more than 10 feet on their next turn.

On a success: half damage, no other effects.

14.name: Denied
14.html:
Your area control has reached its terminal expression. Once per long rest, as an action, you can declare an area Denied — a 60-foot radius that exists for 10 minutes. Within the Denied area: - No creature (friend or foe) can take the Dash action. - Any creature that attempts to enter the area from outside must make an EDGE resistance check (DC = 8 + proficiency + Wisdom modifier) or take 6d8 resonant damage and be knocked prone at the entry point. - You can move freely through the Denied area without affecting yourself.

---

system.description: The Iron Suppressor has built their practice around area denial and crowd control — not the elimination of single targets but the systematic removal of every option from a large number of targets simultaneously. They are the reason that coordinated enemy movement becomes impossible, that organized formations collapse into survival behavior, and that tactical plans fail at the execution stage because the execution space has been turned into somewhere no one wants to be. They are the most strategically valuable Pulse Caller in large-scale engagements and the most exhausting to fight alongside in small ones.
